1973. 

And then came the silence.

Not days. Not months.
35 years in a Pakistani prison.

Torture. Interrogation. Isolation.

Not once.

When he returned in 2008, he didn’t just come back as a man…
he came back as a reminder:

The most dangerous warriors don’t fight in the open…
they disappear in the shadows.

There are many such forgotten heroes.

Viewed Yesterday I went out to deliver a few Plant Protein orders of myself.

We had a delay in shipping and I wanted to apologise to some of the people who had been waiting.

What I didn’t expect was the warmth. At every door I knocked on, people welcomed me with so much kindness.

I love doing these visits because they help me connect with our customers beyond the screen, on a more personal level, and also get more detailed feedback.

Most companies today look at customers as numbers, statistics or targets like views, orders and revenue.

But behind every order or every rupee of revenue is a person who trusted you enough to try something you built.

That is why at Only What's Needed I don’t believe businesses are really D2C or B2B.

At the end of the day, everything is H2H. Human to human.

The more we remember this, the better the businesses we build will be.

मानवता के कल्याण को दृष्टिगत रखते हुए उन्होंने सत्य, अहिंसा, अस्तेय, अपरिग्रह और ब्रह्मचर्य के रूप में पाँच सूत्र दिए थे, जिनकी सार्वकालिक प्रासंगिकता है। 

भगवान महावीर ने नारीशक्ति को सम्मानजनक स्थान प्रदान करते हुए उन्हें खोया हुआ गौरव लौटा कर समाज में लैंगिक भेदभाव मिटाने का युगांतरकारी कार्य किया। 

हमारी वर्तमान जीवन शैली से पर्यावरण को हो रही हानि से उसे बचाने में अपरिग्रह का सूत्र बहुत महत्वपूर्ण है। अहिंसा, सह-अस्तित्व और प्राणिमात्र में समान आत्मतत्व के दर्शन करने की उनकी शिक्षा का अनुपालन विश्व के अस्तित्व के लिए परम आवश्यक है।

Viewed Ok, So I am again here after 3 days of getting 33 comments on my previous post about which should I buy between Neo and Loq, I got a ton of advice now I want one more suggestion.

As you'll mentioned earlier in almost every comment that I should go for Air M2 instead of loq which would increase some price bracket, offers much more. So is it good to have a Air M2 Now, while having a better Nvidia Gpu till 4070 from the same price point.

Loq have processors till Amd Ryzen 7 and Intel i7 hx. But Air M2 is better from both of these but...

Because of the lesser performance cores and overall power push M2 have, Nvidia can push it on the booting of the laptop ? 

This is totally on my use case also which requires sometimes a good battery life/ Heavy power required tasks. My mostly work is going to be in the classrooms which may have some power sockets to charge it on with loq but with Air M2 the problem of specifically battery isn't there but again when I want to have a heavy task running in Air M2 for like ½-1 hour of simulation, coding, testing or debugging any heavy software, M2 will not stand a chance against Loq in any way possible..There is also another option for Asus Tuf though ??? What do you guys think now ???Peace and sorry for this much info and yappin

Viewed शारदा विश्वविद्यालय में आरएसएस की प्रमुख जन गोष्ठी संपन्न

राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघ (RSS) के शताब्दी वर्ष के उपलक्ष्य में जिला गौतमबुद्ध नगर के शारदा विश्वविद्यालय में एक प्रमुख जनगोष्ठी का आयोजन किया गया। कार्यक्रम की शुरुआत दीप प्रज्ज्वलन से हुई। कार्यक्रम में मुख्य वक्ता आनंद जी (सह प्रांत प्रचारक, मेरठ प्रांत) ने संघ की 100 वर्ष की यात्रा पर प्रकाश डालते हुए राष्ट्र निर्माण और संगठन में संघ की भूमिका को रेखांकित किया।

उन्होंने बताया कि आज़ादी के बाद से संघ ने एक स्थाई और सशक्त भारत के निर्माण में योगदान दिया है, जो विश्व का मार्गदर्शन कर सके। उन्होंने गोवा मुक्ति आंदोलन से लेकर विभिन्न युद्धों तक स्वयंसेवकों के साहस और त्याग का उल्लेख किया और संगठित हिंदू समाज की भूमिका पर बल दिया।

कार्यक्रम में प्रश्नोत्तरी के माध्यम से प्रमुखजनों द्वारा वर्तमान में देश में जनसंख्या असंतुलन में होने वाले परिवर्तनों और घुसपैठियों की समस्या पर आनंद जी ने अपने विचार रखे।

अधिवक्ता मीरा चौधरी जी ने भारतीय संस्कृति में नारी शक्ति की पूजनीय भूमिका पर विचार रखते हुए कहा कि नारी को अपने को कमजोर नहीं समझना चाहिए। उन्होंने कहा कि "Man makes society, but women makes man" और यह आह्वान किया कि नारी का श्रृंगार गहनों के साथ-साथ शस्त्र धारण करने से ही पूर्ण होता है।

महेश गुप्ता जी (राष्ट्रीय अध्यक्ष, भारत विकास परिषद) ने संघ स्थापना के बाद स्वयंसेवकों द्वारा झेली गई प्रताड़नाओं के बावजूद समाज जागरण और शाखा के माध्यम से व्यक्ति निर्माण के प्रयासों पर प्रकाश डाला। उन्होंने श्री राम जन्मभूमि आंदोलन में संघ की भूमिका का उल्लेख करते हुए कहा कि हिंदू समाज को जाति भाव से ऊपर उठकर हिंदू भाव से आगे बढ़ना होगा। उन्होंने पंच परिवर्तन सामाजिक समरसता, कुटुंब प्रबोधन, पर्यावरण संरक्षण, आत्मबोध और नागरिक कर्तव्य को अपनाने पर जोर दिया और जल, जंगल और जमीन बचाने के लिए विशेष प्रयास का आह्वान किया।

इस जनगोष्ठी में समाज के प्रमुख जनों, सज्जन शक्ति और मातृ शक्ति की बड़ी संख्या में उपस्थिति रही।

राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघ (RSS) के शताब्दी वर्ष (2025–26) के उपलक्ष्य में आज जिला गौतमबुद्ध नगर के शारदा विश्वविद्यालय में एक प्रमुख जनगोष्ठी का आयोजन किया गया। कार्यक्रम की शुरुआत दीप प्रज्ज्वलन से हुई। कार्यक्रम में मुख्य वक्ता आनंद जी (सह प्रांत प्रचारक, मेरठ प्रांत) ने संघ की 100 वर्ष की यात्रा पर प्रकाश डालते हुए राष्ट्र निर्माण और संगठन में संघ की भूमिका को रेखांकित किया। 

उन्होंने बताया कि आज़ादी के बाद से संघ ने एक स्थायी और सशक्त भारत के निर्माण में योगदान दिया है, जो विश्व का मार्गदर्शन कर सके। उन्होंने गोवा मुक्ति आंदोलन से लेकर विभिन्न युद्धों तक स्वयंसेवकों के साहस और त्याग का उल्लेख किया और संगठित हिंदू समाज की भूमिका पर बल दिया।

Viewed Most product decisions are not about right vs wrong. They’re tradeoffs. 

When we decided to go with pouches for our Only What's Needed protein powder, this was the tradeoff we faced:

Jars are easier to scoop from. Less messy.
But they take more space and increase plastic use.

Pouches are compact. Easier to store.
But slightly more inconvenient to use daily.

We went with pouches, because it helped us keep things simpler and more efficient.

But this is not something we want to decide alone. You’re the one using it every day.

So before we go further, I want to understand your preference.

What would you choose? Vote below 👇

Viewed We can finally say it!
WE WON OUR FIRST INTERCONTINENTAL AWARD IN JAPAN for our viral videos created there—content that resonated with and was recognized by the Japanese community.
After spending two weeks in Japan, we learned two things. First, that Japanese culture is incredibly rich, beautiful, and easy to fall in love with.
Second… Japanese do not break spaghetti (unless your name starts with B and ends with -ayashi.)
We are grateful to JNSCA for this award.

We also had the honor of attending the Tokyo film festival and doing our very first red carpet in Tokyo. Emiliano asked if he could wear his Italy jersey but the answer was “いいえ、できません。” which we assumed it meant no.

Grateful beyond words ❤️

Viewed उदयपुर : अखिल भारतीय योग एवं नैपुण्य वर्ग का शुभारम्भ  



राष्ट्रीय स्वयंसेवक संघ का अखिल भारतीय योग एवं नैपुण्य वर्ग का आयोजन विद्यापीठ विश्वविद्यालय परिसर में किया जा रहा है, 22 मार्च से 26 मार्च तक पांच दिवसीय वर्ग के आयोजन का उद्देश्य योग, शारीरिक दक्षता एवं व्यक्तित्व निर्माण के माध्यम से राष्ट्र निर्माण की भावना को सुदृढ़ करना है। 



अखिल भारतीय शारीरिक शिक्षण प्रमुख जगदीश प्रसाद ने एक योग प्रदर्शनी का लोकार्पण किया। प्रदर्शनी में भगवान शिव, ऋषि पतंजलि, गुरु गोरखनाथ, आदि शंकराचार्य, महर्षि दयानंद सरस्वती, महर्षि अरविंद, पंडित श्रीराम शर्मा आचार्य, महाराज चतुर सिंह बावजी, महात्मा भूरी बाई सहित कुल 18 महान विभूतियों के सचित्र जीवन परिचय प्रदर्शित किए गए हैं। 



सभी महापुरुषों ने अपने जीवन को योग साधना एवं मानव कल्याण के लिए समर्पित किया तथा योग के क्षेत्र में महत्वपूर्ण योगदान दिया। वर्ग में देशभर से आए कुल 90 प्रतिभागी योग एवं आसन का प्रशिक्षण प्राप्त करेंगे।

Viewed Many of us spend time planning investments, building portfolios, and thinking about long-term wealth. But sometimes the most important financial decision is simply protecting the people who protected us all our lives.

For most of our childhood, our parents were the ones worrying about us, our school, our health, our future. But there comes a point when the roles slowly start reversing.

Medical costs today are extremely unpredictable. A single hospitalization can cost several lakhs, and unfortunately, these things often happen suddenly, without warning. The last thing any family should have to worry about in such moments is how to arrange money quickly.

That’s why I genuinely feel health insurance for parents is not just a financial product, it’s peace of mind. For a premium of just ₹700 to ₹900 per month, you can take coverage of ₹10-20L for your family.  And now is the Perfect timing because Govt removed GST on health insurance  saving 18% on your premiums!

But 25+ companies, 100s of plans... how to choose, right? What's covered/excluded? Also, buying directly without knowing what's covered/excluded leads to claims rejection.

That’s why I’d suggest buying Health insurance after consulting an advisor from Ditto. They’re backed by Zerodha. and they give honest and spam-free insurance advice. The best part is that their consultation, calls, and claim support are completely FREE.

Even better, if your insurance claim ever gets denied, Ditto will fight on your behalf, helping with paperwork and providing 24×7 claim support. Unlike many insurance agents, You can take your time, understand the policies properly, and buy the plan only when you feel comfortable.

WE WON OUR FIRST INTERCONTINENTAL AWARD IN JAPAN for our viral videos created there—content that resonated with and was recognized by the Japanese community.
After spending two weeks in Japan, we learned two things. First, that Japanese culture is incredibly rich, beautiful, and easy to fall in love with.
Second… Japanese do not break spaghetti (unless your name starts with B and ends with -ayashi.)
We are grateful to JNSCA for this award.

We also had the honor of attending the Tokyo film festival and doing our very first red carpet in Tokyo. Emiliano asked if he could wear his Italy jersey but the answer was “いいえ、できません。” which we assumed it meant no.

Grateful beyond words ❤️

Viewed This is the riskiest video I’ve ever made! 
20 lakh people have already seen it. 

Usually, I talk about 1 brand per video. But this time, it’s different. In my latest YouTube video, I have exposed 30+ companies and their marketing gimmicks, all in one place.

Lawyers ask me to blur logos, but I never blur logos because India allows freedom of speech and freedom of speech includes the freedom to criticize. 

If a movie critic can review a movie, then why can’t a food critic review a food item? 

That is why I don’t say noodles, I say Maggi. I don’t say soft drink, I say Coke and Pepsi. 

If we want to truly create a health revolution, we need to take brand names openly. 

So if you care about what you and your family are eating, I strongly recommend you watch this.

Video link here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eREIfwS6Stw&t=3s

People deserve to know the truth about these companies.

Let’s continue the fight against misleading marketing.

Viewed “Governments will come and go. Political parties will rise and fall. But this country should remain strong.”
— Atal Bihari Vajpayee

India’s nuclear journey is not the story of one leader or one party. It is the story of national continuity.

We are safe today because decades ago leaders invested in science, defense research, and strategic thinking. Jawaharlal Nehru built the scientific foundation and protected research institutions. Homi Bhabha created the nuclear roadmap. Vikram Sarabhai strengthened India’s scientific ecosystem. Indira Gandhi approved the first nuclear test in 1974 despite global pressure. Atal Bihari Vajpayee declared India a nuclear weapons state in 1998. Later governments strengthened diplomacy and global nuclear recognition.

Each generation carried the responsibility forward.

Nuclear strength did not appear in one moment. It was built patiently through institutions, research, discipline, and political courage.

But there is something more important.

A nation divided weakens itself. When we fight each other internally, we forget the larger picture. Strategic strength protects us from external threats, but unity protects us from internal collapse.

If we divide ourselves, we fall.
If we stand together, we remain strong.

India’s security today exists because leaders across decades placed the nation above party lines at critical moments. Governments changed. The country remained.

That is the lesson.

Viewed Dear audience/subscribers
Years ago i had the idea, a story that i wanted
to explore but whenever i pitched it or imagined people told me 
“Its too big” “its impossible” or “its too ambitious”
All i would get to hear is how its a dream i can never achieve cause Indian audiences never love sci-fi or alien concepts that are villainy/negative
I always wanted a sci fi Indian story thats deeply connected to what our country has to offer

Today after giving almost 3 years to this project i am very happy to say that please never ever be afraid to dream big, never think lesser about yourself and never let anyone tell you that your Ideas are too big for this world.

I wanna thank my ACV team my co-actors & the entire crew of EKAKI for joining me on this massive bumpy turmoil of a journey that we had taken
It all nearly broke us, there were tears there were disappointments and sleepless night but every single day was worth this day, all those years all the budget was worth all your love that you showered❤️
 TO DREAMING BIG EVERYONE 🥂

Viewed Edit:Listen everyone, I am not judging what any couple chooses to do. But let us be rational and logical. Why do we spend huge amounts of money on weddings? Some people spend 2, 3, 8, even 20 lakhs on a single night. Later their financial future starts collapsing. They pay EMIs for years just because of one event. Is that really a wise decision?

Are we doing this only because of blind traditions and what society thinks? Why are we still trying to impress society? Marriage is between two people, not between two families trying to prove something to society. If a couple is not financially strong and they choose a simple wedding so they can build a better future, that is a smart decision.

And let us be honest. Even if you arrange a lavish wedding to impress people, they will still criticize something. They will complain about the food, the decoration, the venue, or anything else. Society will always have an opinion. You cannot please everyone.

If we keep following old customs only because past generations did it, then how will we move forward? Traditions are not meant to trap us. They are meant to evolve. Marriage should be about love, partnership, respect, and a peaceful life together. It is not a competition or a public show.

Focus on your life and your future, not on what people think. As shown in the Rajesh Khanna movie song “Kuch To Log Kahenge” from the film Amar Prem starring Rajesh Khanna, people will always talk. That is what they do.

What matters is whether the two people getting married are happy and secure. The rest is just noise.


Society has always loved drama more than wisdom. But financial stability, emotional maturity, and mutual respect are far more powerful than a one night celebration. In the long run, compounding interest works harder than social approval ever will.
A couple has drawn attention online after choosing to skip an extravagant wedding celebration and instead invest their savings in buying a new home. The pair decided to solemnise their marriage inside their newly purchased flat, keeping the ceremony simple and intimate.

According to reports, the couple prioritised long-term financial stability over spending large sums on a traditional, lavish wedding. The ceremony was attended by close family members and conducted with customary rituals inside the decorated home, symbolising the beginning of their married life in their own space.

Their decision has sparked conversations on social media, with many praising the practical approach and highlighting the growing trend among young couples to focus on financial security and meaningful celebrations rather than costly wedding events.

Viewed Holi is almost here.

And behind all the colors and celebration lies one of the most powerful stories in our tradition - the story of Lord Narasimha and Prahlad Maharaj.

In this episode with Dhruva Gorrick, we didn’t just retell the legend. We went deep into what it truly means - devotion in the face of fear, ego versus faith, and why the divine appeared in such a fierce form to protect a devotee.

My intention with this conversation was simple: to help you understand one of the most important stories in Sanatan Dharma in detail, and in spirit, especially as we approach Holi.

Before you celebrate this year, take a moment to reflect… what does this story mean to you?

Viewed It was a long time wish of mine to meet with (& work with) Farah Khan!

Loved her movies...

But the reason I wanted to meet her was because of her IMMENSE ability to reinvent herself.

The one media SKILL that's kept my career going for 10 years.

On the day of the shoot, I learnt it from the queen of reinvention herself! So many lessons shared off camera. But here's (the tiniest) gist :-

10 years in media have taught me the following :
- Nothing is permanent. Don't fly too high, don't sink too deep. Keep walking.
- Artists are paid to keep producing art. An artist therefore, must focus on their mental health. Sleep well, laugh a lot & stay in shape :)
- Your work is an outcome of how much fun you're having. First & foremost, focus on internal "Rhythm".

As a great being once said, "Bakch*di barkarar rahe!"

First entertain yourself, then entertain the world 🌍

Talk shit, keep growing 😉

Viewed Over 350 Women Trapped and Exploited in Forced Conversion and Prostitution Network in Basti

Authorities have uncovered disturbing details of a network that allegedly trapped and exploited hundreds of women in Basti, Uttar Pradesh. According to findings and victim statements, the operation involved deception, coercion, and systematic abuse.

The accused reportedly targeted women by posing as a Hindu man and approaching them using fake names and false identities. Victims were lured through promises of marriage, job opportunities, and financial support. Over time, private relationships were developed, and the women were gradually brought under control.

A key location used in this operation was a little known lodge, locally referred to as a “publicing lounge.” Women were taken there without any identification checks. The facility reportedly had no reception desk, no Aadhaar or ID verification system, and no guest registers, making it extremely difficult to track who stayed there.

The layout of the lodge raised serious concerns. It reportedly included small rooms, a room constructed inside another room, and narrow alleyways that allowed quick movement and escape if trouble arose. These structural features made surveillance and accountability nearly impossible.

Victims have stated that private relations were secretly recorded and later used to threaten and control them. Several women also revealed that there was pressure related to religious conversion, which they say was part of the larger pattern of exploitation.

It is claimed that more than 350 women may have been affected by this network. Some victims are still missing, and others are reportedly too frightened to speak publicly.

The case has raised serious questions about illegal lodging practices, lack of oversight, and how such operations continued without detection for a long time. Investigations are ongoing to identify all those involved and to locate missing victims.

Viewed The President of the French Republic, H.E. Emmanuel Macron, was on Figuring Out.

It was an honour to host him.
But beyond the honour, we see this as a responsibility.

Because when someone like President Macron chooses to have a conversation with us, it becomes an opportunity to talk about India on a global stage.

What we're getting right.
Where we're struggling.
What the world needs to understand about us.

"The question is no longer whether India innovates. The question is who will innovate with India."

That's what the French President had to say about India.

The world's posture towards India has changed.

We're not the market they want to sell to anymore.
We're the partner they want to build with.

We talked about UPI, Aadhaar, and the way India leapfrogged legacy systems that other countries are still stuck with.

President Macron was surprisingly honest about where France went wrong.

A country that invented cinema, photography, and the hot air balloon and yet today, the largest tech companies in the world are either American or Chinese.

He admitted it openly. He said Europe's problem wasn't ideas, it was scale, capital, and appetite for risk. Three things. All three missing.

And then we talked about leadership. About respect. About what happens when private conversations between presidents end up on social media.

These are the kind of conversations we always hoped this platform could have.

And thanks to all of you who watch us, your belief in what we're building, your support, Figuring Out is able to host such guests.

Viewed Untold Story of Two Corruption-Free Parsi Brothers Who Lost Because of Honesty

Pune:
In the 1990s, a stretch of road in Pune quietly became an example of what honest work in India often leads to. Jangli Maharaj Road was built by two Parsi brothers, engineers who ran a company named Recondo. They did not cut corners, they did not pay bribes, and they did not follow the informal rules of the system. They simply built the road properly.

The construction followed textbook engineering standards. The foundation was strong, drainage was planned correctly, and materials were used in the right proportions. There were no shortcuts. The result was unusual for its time. The road lasted for years without needing repeated repairs.

In a fair system, such work would have brought recognition and more contracts. In reality, it did the opposite.

Road construction in India has long functioned on a repair cycle. Roads break frequently, repairs are sanctioned, new tenders are issued, and commissions flow through the system. Poor quality creates continuous work and continuous income. A well-built road disrupts this cycle.

By building one durable road, the two brothers unintentionally ended future repair work on that stretch. With no repairs, there were no tenders. With no tenders, there were no commissions. Their success became a liability.

After completing the project, the brothers stopped receiving government contracts. There was no official rejection and no written explanation. Their files simply stopped moving. Meetings led nowhere. Tenders went to others. The exclusion was silent but effective.

The company eventually shut down. The brothers left India and continued their professional lives abroad.

Their story reflects a deeper problem. India does not fail because of lack of talent or skill. It fails when systems reward corruption and punish competence. When honest professionals are pushed out, mediocrity becomes the norm. When good work is blocked, bad work becomes routine.

This is why roads break repeatedly. This is why capable people leave. This is why progress feels slower than it should be.

The story of the two Parsi brothers is not widely recorded, but it survives through memory because it exposes an uncomfortable truth. In many parts of the system, honesty is not enough to succeed.

Corruption is not a side issue in India’s development. It remains one of the core reasons honest work disappears quietly.
